w:r.w:delText: AI estimates fill %; precise volume calculated from known bottle geometry

w:r.w:t: The 'Ruler vs. Guesser' Approach: AI acts as a digital ruler to find fill percentage, while the bottle registry anchors results to 100% precise physics and geometry.
Every scan is a labeled training example. The product IS the data engine
$0/month infrastructure on Cloudflare + Gemini free tiers

User's phone captures and compresses photo (800px JPEG, ~70KB), sends to Cloudflare Worker edge proxy. The worker validates origin, checks rate limits, then routes to Gemini 2.5 Flash (with Groq online-only auto-fallback). AI returns fill percentage and confidence. Client-side calculates precise volumes from known bottle geometry and USDA nutrition data. Note: In POC, R2 storage is deferred (credit card required) — images are processed but not persisted.

Pattern: Thin-Client PWA + Serverless Edge Proxy + Object Storage. All domain logic (volume calculation, unit conversion, nutrition lookup) runs in the PWA client. The Cloudflare Worker is a security proxy with data persistence responsibilities. No application server. No database server.
QR scan → camera → compress to 800px JPEG (~70KB) → POST to Worker. All volume/nutrition logic runs locally.
Origin validation · Rate limiting (10 req/min/IP) · Payload guard (<4MB) · LLM routing (Gemini → Groq) · R2 storage binding
Receives base64 JPEG + SKU context. Returns JSON: { fill_percentage, confidence }. Free: 500 req/day.
Online-only fallback. OpenAI-compatible API. Auto-failover if Gemini fails. Post-POC: replace with fine-tuned Qwen2.5-VL 7B (offline-capable).
images/{scanId}.jpg + metadata/{scanId}.json. 10 GB free (~140K scans). $0 egress. ⚠ Requires credit card activation — deferred to post-POC.
Unlimited bandwidth. Custom domains. PR preview deployments. CDN-distributed globally.
Sliding window: 10 requests/min/IP. Stored in KV store (zero latency at edge).
Auto-deploy on push to main. Runs 34 unit tests. Uses wrangler-action@v3 for Worker deploys.
PWA loads bottle geometry from local bottleRegistry.js
User captures photo → canvas → JPEG @ 800px, quality 0.78 (~70KB)
Worker validates origin, rate limit, payload → stores image to R2 (deferred in POC)
Worker stores metadata → returns { scanId, fillPercentage: 42, confidence, provider }
PWA calculates: 500ml × 42% = 210ml remaining, 290ml consumed → 14.2 tbsp, 0.89 cups
User gives feedback → POST /feedback → Worker validates → updates metadata/{scanId}.json
Cloudflare Workers run on V8 isolates, not containers
Gemini/Groq keys in Worker env vars only
All serverless. All free tier. Zero cold starts. Zero egress fees. No database server. No application server.
9 screens designed: QR Landing → Camera Viewfinder → Photo Preview → Analyzing State → Result Display → Feedback → Confirmation → Error States → iOS Warning
Fill gauge with animated bottle visualization
Safi Oil Tracker uses a Progressive Web App (PWA) approach for both POC and Full Launch. A native mobile app is a Phase 4 option after 1,000+ scans validate the concept.
Must use Safari browser mode (WebKit camera bug in standalone PWA)
Decision: PWA for POC + Full Launch. Native only after 1,000+ scans prove market fit and justify the higher development cost.
Every scan automatically becomes a labeled training example. User feedback is validated through 4 sanity checks before being accepted as training data.
Prompt refinement based on error patterns
Few-shot examples from best labeled pairs
Fine-tune Gemini Flash via Google AI Studio
Secrets in Cloudflare Worker only (never in client/git)
No PII collected. No accounts, no names, no emails. R2 bucket not publicly accessible.
34 tests passing · auto-deploy on every push
Entire stack on free tiers · Valid for up to ~15,000 scans/month
Unlimited bandwidth, custom domains, PR previews
10 GB storage · ~140K scans · $0 egress · Requires credit card activation
Once volume exceeds free tier limits, costs scale linearly and remain very low. At 10,000–100,000 scans/month:
~1,550 tokens/scan (1,500 input + 50 output) using Gemini 2.5 Flash:
Cost Per Scan (Gemini 2.5 Flash — Paid Tier)
~1,500 input tokens + ~50 output tokens per scan
At 100,000 scans: ~$26 in AI token costs alone. Storage + compute add ~$10–20.
No direct competitor exists for AI oil tracking via QR + camera.
High friction, user must type every entry
Scans labels, not actual content levels
Safi Oil Tracker = Zero friction + Zero hardware + Self-improving AI. An uncontested niche.
READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION ✔ — All planning artifacts complete and verified.
iOS camera bug in standalone PWA
Browser mode enforced; "Open in Safari" fallback
POC scoped to clear glass; ±15% framing
Disabled AI thinking; 800px compression; Groq fallback
Auto-fallback to Groq; per-IP rate limiting
— Client sends bottle images directly (no QR step needed). Split into training set + held-out test set to measure baseline AI accuracy.
— Iteratively improve Gemini prompts using test results. Basic prompt-refinement loop works with sample data, no R2 or credit card needed.
Deploy + Validate QR Code Scan Flow
— Push to Cloudflare Pages + Worker. Print QR codes for 3 SKUs. Verify end-to-end flow (standard functionality, no innovation risk).
— Live scan collection with feedback. Each scan produces a labeled training pair (image + user-corrected fill level).
Go / No-Go Decision: Scale Beyond POC
— If AI accuracy hits ≤15% MAE and feedback rate ≥30%: greenlight Phase 2 (activate R2, add user accounts, scan history, notifications, more SKUs; $0–5/mo added). If not: iterate prompts or pivot.
